channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: White House acknowledges disagreement with Jordan over relocating Palestinians
item.description: The White House on Wednesday acknowledged disagreement between President Trump and King Abdullah II of Jordon over Trump's pitch to relocate Palestinians from the Gaza Strip while the U.S. takes over the territory.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Nevada Legislature OKs bill targeting high egg prices, allowing suspension of cage-free requirement
item.description: Nevada lawmakers on Wednesday gave final approval to a bill that could bring down the price of eggs, clearing the way for Gov. Joe Lombardo to give it his signature.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: USAID staffers describe colleagues abandoned in violence in Congo as DOGE ends assistance programs
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A lieutenant of Trump ally Elon Musk and other outsiders are overseeing the immediate termination of hundreds of American aid and foreign assistance programs abroad this week, without required documentation or justification, according to newly filed affidavits from staffers and acc
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate confirms Gabbard to serve as nation’s top intelligence chief
item.description: The Senate voted largely along party lines Wednesday to confirm Tulsi Gabbard to serve as the director of national intelligence, the nation’s top intelligence official, despite strong objections from Democrats and initial misgivings from Republicans who questioned her experience and judgment.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Federal health agencies restore webpages and datasets following judge's order
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Federal health agencies have restored several webpages and datasets, following a judge's order to bring back public access to information that had been removed to comply with a presidential executive order.
item.dc:creator: MIKE STOBBE and KASTURI PANANJADY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US defense chief Hegseth calls NATO membership for Ukraine unrealistic
item.description: Hegseth made the comments during the first trip to NATO and the Ukraine Defense Contact Group by a member of the new Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: LORNE COOK and TARA COPP, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Modi and Trump's friendly rapport may be tested as Indian prime minister visits Washington
item.description: NEW DELHI (AP) — Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s longstanding bonhomie with President Donald Trump could be tested as the Indian leader kicks off a visit to Washington on Wednesday, eager to avoid tariffs that have been slapped on others and threats of further taxes and imports.
item.dc:creator: ASHOK SHARMA and JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: With federal money at risk, Minnesota’s public colleges stress high stakes of state budget debate
item.description: The University of Minnesota and Minnesota State Colleges and Universities systems are hoping for extra allocations from the Legislature this year. They know it’s a challenge given the state’s leaner budget.
item.dc:creator: Peter Cox, MPR News

item.title: It’s the last week to change party affiliation ahead of NYC mayor’s race
item.description: NEW YORK CITY (PIX11) – Friday is the last day to change your party registration ahead of the 2025 mayoral election. In June, New York City voters will pick their party’s candidate for the mayoral election in November.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, PIX 11

item.title: What’s next for Jake Sullivan, a Minnesotan and Biden’s former National Security Advisor
item.description: As National Security Advisor, Jake Sullivan helped shape Biden’s foreign policy agenda and was involved in the U.S. response to events like the Russian invasion of Ukraine and the Israel-Hamas war.
item.dc:creator: Nina Moini and Alanna Elder, MPR News

item.title: Durbin calls for investigation into Patel's role in FBI firings
item.description: Sen. Dick Durbin (D-Ill.) is asking the Department of Justice’s (DOJ) inspector general to investigate “highly credible information” that Kash Patel, President Trump’s pick to head the FBI, is already directing a “purge” of the bureau before he’s been confirmed by the Senate.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: PBS closes DEI office to comply with executive order
item.description: PBS' move comes as Trump's FCC also continues to turn up the heat on public broadcasters over taxpayer funding and editorial direction.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, NewsNation

item.title: Minnesota nonprofits ride waves of tumult from Trump's spending freeze attempts
item.description: President Donald Trump’s federal spending freeze was recently blocked by a judge, but the tumult leaves Minnesota’s nearly 9,000 nonprofits and tax-exempt entities that receive federal money wondering what’s next.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer, Gracie Stockton, and Lukas Levin, MPR News

item.title: Vance to attend international AI and security summits as Ukraine war and tariffs loom
item.description: Vance will likely use the summits to resist further AI regulation while reiterating a common Trump refrain: that military allies need to further ramp up spending that supports NATO and Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Willem Marx, MPR News

item.title: A deep dive on U.S. reading and math scores, and what to do about them
item.description: New research drills down on poor student achievement, and notes some school districts that are beating the odds.
item.dc:creator: Cory Turner, MPR News

item.title: An Ely group agrees on the value of the Boundary Waters
item.description: Whether they’re conservative or liberal, people in Ely seem to agree on one thing: they love the Boundary Waters. What they can’t seem to agree on, though, is whether copper-nickel mining would endanger those beloved lakes.
item.dc:creator: Catharine Richert, MPR News

item.title: Egg price bill advances at Nevada Legislature; AB171 could increase supply in 30 days
item.description: A bill aimed at reducing the skyrocketing price of eggs in Nevada advanced on Monday, but the legislative process will take some time even if there's no opposition.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Florida lawmakers to begin third special session on illegal immigration
item.description: After a particularly contentious few weeks, Florida lawmakers will be heading back to Tallahassee Tuesday for the purpose of passing legislation on illegal immigration — this time with the governor's support.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Rains, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Battleground Florida: Officials discuss tariffs, LGBTQ policies
item.description: University of Tampa Economics Professor Michael Coon explained the purpose of tariffs and the impact they will likely have on businesses and families in Tampa Bay.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump fires Office of Government Ethics chief
item.description: President Trump has fired the director of the Office of Government Ethics, the agency announced Monday. The office posted on its website that it had been notified Trump was removing David Huitema, who had been nominated by former President Biden.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WFLA, NBC

item.title: HIV infections could jump over 6 times if US support dropped & not replaced, UNAIDS chief says
item.description: The head of the U.N. AIDS agency said Monday the number of new HIV infections could jump more than six times by 2029 if American support of the biggest AIDS program is dropped, warning that millions of people could die and more resistant strains of the disease could emerge.
item.dc:creator: MARIA CHENG AP Medical Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Black educators warn of what is lost if Education Department is shuttered
item.description: Black educators are sounding the alarm as President Donald Trump ramps up talk of eliminating the Department of Education.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, Fox 5

item.title: GOP senators 'terrified' of crossing Trump, facing Musk-funded challengers
item.description: GOP senators are "terrified" over the prospect of facing primary challengers funded by Elon Musk if they stick their necks out by opposing President Donald Trump's agenda.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, Fox 5

item.title: DeSantis backs Trump’s plans for FEMA, DOGE efforts
item.description: The governor announced his support for President Donald Trump looking to remove FEMA as a middleman after a disaster.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Tampa Bay lawmaker pushes bold legislation to protect homeownership from corporations
item.description: In what's being called a "bold new measure," one Tampa Bay lawmaker wants to put families over corporate elites by making sure local Floridians can stay in their neighborhoods.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: DOGE was tasked with stopping Treasury payments to USAID, AP sources say
item.description: Officials working with Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency sought access to the U.S. Department of Treasury payment system to stop money from flowing to the U.S. Agency for International Development, according to two people familiar with the matter.
item.dc:creator: WFLA, NBC
